content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed the `materialize_beads` feature branch: `git diff` against master, the service/bundle/adapter/cli implementation files, and ran the bead-related unit tests (all 15 `materialize_beads_*` tests pass; `cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings` and `cargo fmt --check` are clean). The implementation correctly builds the root-epic → workstream-epic → task-bead hierarchy with `ParentChild` and `Blocks` dependencies, attaches planning comments idempotently, honors the milestone scope label for reuse, guards concurrent exports with a PID-liveness file lock, and records a `ProgressUpdated{sub_type=beads_exported}` journal event whose `planned_bead_id_map` is merged back into `load_runtime_bead_membership_refs`. One unrelated pre-existing test (`cli::run::tests::resume_running_failed_project_reenters_failure_reconciliation`) also fails on master (`d3704ae6`) and is not caused by this branch. Two real correctness concerns worth fixing before merge: (1) `create_bead` now joins labels with `,` into a single `--labels=` arg, so a plan label containing a comma would be silently split; bundle/adapter validation doesn't catch this. (2) `find_existing_materialized_bead` silently reuses a single historical (possibly closed) match while erroring on the 2-historical case, which is asymmetric and will resurrect closed beads during re-export.

## Amendments

1. In `src/adapters/br_process.rs:1341-1344`, `BrMutationAdapter::create_bead` joins labels with `,` and passes them as a single `--labels=a,b,c` argument. Bundle validation (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:205-207`) and the materialization label builder only reject empty labels — a label value containing a literal `,` would be silently split into two labels by the `br` CLI. Since bead label strings flow from the (often LLM-authored) plan bundle through `materialized_bead_labels()` into this adapter unchanged, a stray comma in a plan label would silently corrupt the labels applied to the created bead without any error path catching it. Either reject labels containing `,` in bundle validation, reject them in `create_bead`, or revert to the previous repeated `--label=...` form so label values are passed opaquely.
   Rationale: Silent label corruption from plan input is a correctness/robustness gap in a new adapter-level behavior change. Cheap to fix now (one-line validation or revert the `--labels=` change) and prevents mis-tagged beads that downstream label-based lookups rely on.
2. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2156-2192`, `find_existing_materialized_bead` is inconsistent about historical (non-active) matches: 2+ active matches error (`multiple active milestone-scoped beads already match title`), 2+ historical matches error (`has multiple historical matches`), but **exactly one historical match is silently reused** (`else if eligible_indices.len() == 1 { eligible_indices[0] }`). This means a bead that was closed/cancelled for this milestone will be reused by a subsequent `export-beads` run instead of a fresh one being created, without any status check or warning — even though closed beads are presumably not usable work items. This is also asymmetric with the handling further down (an active-match with incompatible metadata errors out). Either require the single reused match to be active, or make this branch explicit about accepting historical matches (and document why), to avoid confusing the downstream readiness computation which treats historical beads as live.
   Rationale: Idempotency is a stated acceptance criterion and this branch is the center of that logic. The current behavior silently resurrects closed beads into an exported plan, which is subtle and will cause confusing states during manual bead cleanup (close + re-export). A short status filter (plus a comment explaining the chosen policy) removes the surprise without changing the test suite semantics.
